lines: QUESTION ID 62500 : sgries 3 - CFTC / NFA Regulations (wrong)
What conditions must be met for an associated person's temporary license (TL) to remain valid after its issuance?

A. The temporary license will remain valid for six months, but it will automatically terminate if the sponsoring member terminates the AP or if the AP fails to submit a fingerprint card within 20 days of issuance.
8. The TL is valid until the NFA completes its background check on the AP, which typically takes at least 60 days, after which the license will either be approved or revoked.
C. The associated person must demonstrate ongoing compliance with NFA rules and regulations throughout the duration of the TL; failing to comply will lead to an immediate termination of the license.
D. The TL automatically extends for an additional six months if the AP submits a new Form 8-R before the initial six-month period concludes, ensuring uninterrupted registration status.

For a temporary license (TL) to remain valid, it must be within the six-month timeframe, and it will be automatically terminated if the sponsoring member terminates the AP or if the AP fails to submit a fingerprint card within 20 days of issuance.

text:

the following is correct?
A. At the open the exchange will increase the daily price limit only
8. At the open the exchange will increase the daily price limit and the margin requirement
C. At the open the exchange will increase the margin requirement only
D. The exchange will only increase the daily price limit and the margin requirement if the commodity remains in a state of limit up at the close of the domestic session

close of the domestic session.

lines: QUESTION ID IgE71 : serigs g options and spreads
Your customer is short 10 May feeder cattle at 2.83 and has sold 10 1 May feeder cat;e 275 puts at .04. Which of the following is (are) false?
l. The investor's potential loss is limited
Ill. If May feeder cattle rises the investor will have the contracts called away from them
IV. If at expiration May feede callte is at 2.78 the investor will realize a profit

The investor is short futures and short puts. Their potential loss is unlimited. The investor is short puts not calls so the futures contract will not be called away if the contracts rise in Price. The contracts will be put to them if the contracts fall in Price. To determine the
breakeven add the sale price of the futures contract to the premium received from the sale of the puts. ln this case 283 = 287.

lines: A construction company has just been awarded a contract to build a new office complex. The company estimates that the project will require 200,000 pound of copper to complete the required plumbing and

electrical work. The project is to start in March three months from now and it is estimated that the plumbing and electrical work will be starting in June six months from now. Copper in the spot rmarket is $3.05 per

pound and monthly storage and insurance is 3 cents and 1.5 cents per pound respectively. March futures are trading at 3.19 and the June futures are trading at 3.33. Each copper contract covers 25,000 pounds of
copper. What is the best option for the company to hedge the copper cost?
A. Purchase copper in the cash market and store it for 6 months
D. Purchase copper in the cash market and sell g June copper

Given the prices in the spot market and in the March and June futures contracts, the best option for the company is to purchase the copper in the spot market and store it for 6 months. The total cost to buy the copper in
the spot market and to store it is 3.05 plus 27 cents total carrying costs for 6 months or 3.32 per pound. This is one cent less per pound than the cost of the June futures contract at 3.33.
text:
QUESTION ID 19259 : sgries 3 - speculation & Hedging
A construction company has just been awarded a contract to build a new office complex. The company estimates that the project will require 200,000 pound of copper to complete the required plumbing and
electrical work. The project is to start in March three months from now and it is estimated that the plumbing and electrical work will be starting in June six months from now. Copper in the spot rmarket is $3.05 per
pound and monthly storage and insurance is 3 cents and 1.5 cents per pound respectively. March futures are trading at 3.19 and the June futures are trading at 3.33. Each copper contract covers 25,000 pounds of
copper. What is the best option for the company to hedge the copper cost?
A. Purchase copper in the cash market and store it for 6 months
8. Purchase g March copper
C. Purchase g June copper
D. Purchase copper in the cash market and sell g June copper
» Correct Answer: A
» Your Answer: C
Given the prices in the spot market and in the March and June futures contracts, the best option for the company is to purchase the copper in the spot market and store it for 6 months. The total cost to buy the copper in
the spot market and to store it is 3.05 plus 27 cents total carrying costs for 6 months or 3.32 per pound. This is one cent less per pound than the cost of the June futures contract at 3.33.

lines: The use of futures to hedge a long position in an underlying commodity will allow the producer to time their cash market transaction

True - The use of futures to hedge a long position in an underlying commodity will allow the producer to time their cash market transaction. The producer can sell futures with a delivery period that matches the time when
they would like to deliver the cash commodity.
text:
QUESTION ID : serigs - speculation & Hedging
The use of futures to hedge a long position in an underlying commodity will allow the producer to time their cash market transaction
( True/False)
» Correct Answer: A
» Your Answer: B
True - The use of futures to hedge a long position in an underlying commodity will allow the producer to time their cash market transaction. The producer can sell futures with a delivery period that matches the time when
they would like to deliver the cash commodity.

lines: The Federal Reserve is seeking to reduce the level of monetary accommodation by lowering the level of its bond purchases. A trader would most likely want to establish which of the following positions as a result of

When the Federal Reserve reduces the level of monetary accommodation by lowering the level of its bond purchases it will cause the value of the dollar to rise as fewer dollars will be in the system. The price of gold would likely fall as the resuit of the stronger dollar and
reduced stimulus. The trader would not want to go long T' bonds at this time as the price of T' bonds would most likely decline as the demand from the Fed is removed.
text:
QUESTION ID 19214 : series 2 - speculation & Hedging
The Federal Reserve is seeking to reduce the level of monetary accommodation by lowering the level of its bond purchases. A trader would most likely want to establish which of the following positions as a result of
the action by the FRB?
A. Long December T bonds
8. Short October Gold
C. Long March wheat
D. Short September corn
» Correct Answer: B
» Your Answer: C
When the Federal Reserve reduces the level of monetary accommodation by lowering the level of its bond purchases it will cause the value of the dollar to rise as fewer dollars will be in the system. The price of gold would likely fall as the resuit of the stronger dollar and
reduced stimulus. The trader would not want to go long T' bonds at this time as the price of T' bonds would most likely decline as the demand from the Fed is removed.

lines: After a long run up in prices, the price of soybeans have peaked. As the price of soybeans starts to fall, open interest begins to decline, which of the following are true?
l. This is a liquidating rnarket
Il. This is a technically strong market
Ill. Long are offsetting their positions
IV. Short positions are being established at a rate that exceeds the liquidation of the long contracts

All of the choices are correct except IV. Open interest is falling so it indicates that longs are being liquidated faster than new short positions are being established. This makes the market a liquidating market. As prices and
open interest fall it is an indication that the market is technically strong.
text:
QUESTION ID 20ggg : series 3 - Price Forecasting

l. This is a liquidating rnarket
Il. This is a technically strong market
Ill. Long are offsetting their positions
IV. Short positions are being established at a rate that exceeds the liquidation of the long contracts
A. I and Il
8. Il and IV
C. l, Il and Ill
D. l, II, Ill, IV
» Correct Answer: C
» Your Answer: D
All of the choices are correct except IV. Open interest is falling so it indicates that longs are being liquidated faster than new short positions are being established. This makes the market a liquidating market. As prices and
open interest fall it is an indication that the market is technically strong.

lines: To determine open interest you never add open longs and open short positions. Each open futures contract consists of 1 open long position and 1 open short position.

open long and open short position.

lines: A miller needs to purchase 50,000 bushels of corn in the cash market. Cash corn is trading in the spot market at S4.50 per bushel. The costs that must be paid are as follows :
What are the total carrying charges for corn per month on a per bushel basis ?

Shipping is not a cost of carrying a commodity. Storage, interest on borrowed funds and insurance are all calculated in the carrying costs. To determine the interest cost take the price of the commodity and multiply it by the interest rate. ln this case S4.SO x 10/0 —
interest per month per bushel. When the interest costs are added to the storage charge of 3 cents and the insurance charge of 2 cents, we get a total cost to carry corn of 9.5 cents per bushel.

lines: A trader who is looking at the price action of wheat notices that the price of the commodity has rnade a low followed by a rally and a subsequent lower low. Wheat over the last few weeks has rallied again and pulled

Il. This is a very strong buy signal

A head and shoulders bottom is a very strong buy signal and is the reversal of a downtrend. A head and shoulders bottom is very bullish for the price of the commodity exhibiting the pattern.

lines: If one part of the country experiences a change in the demand for a commodity while the demand in all other markets remains constant, a trader would expect a change in the basis for that commodity.

If one part of the country experiences a change in either the supply or demand for a given commodity that change would be expected to change the basis, the price of the cash commodity relative to the near term futures contract.
text:

( True/False)

» Your Answer: B
If one part of the country experiences a change in either the supply or demand for a given commodity that change would be expected to change the basis, the price of the cash commodity relative to the near term futures contract.
